definition: The private sector financial balance is currently in surplus at ~2-3% GDP, derived from the sectoral balance identity (Govt deficit 6-7% = Private surplus 2-3% + Current account deficit 3-3.5%). This makes the current expansion structurally more resilient than 1999-2000 (private deficit ~5% GDP) or 2005-2007 (private deficit ~3-4% GDP).
evidence: Sectoral balance identity is accounting, not theory — arithmetically verifiable
In 1999-2000, private sector was in deficit ~5% GDP; in 2005-2007, deficit ~3-4% GDP
Godley framework correctly predicted both 2001 and 2008 crises from private deficit readings
pair_2 called this the single most valuable analytical contribution across both papers
open_questions: How quickly would the private surplus erode under fiscal consolidation?
Does the surplus distribution matter (concentrated in top decile vs broad-based)?
Is the Godley framework's predictive record robust out of sample?
